description: Next.js cache poisoning chains (Allam 2025, PortSwigger). ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) + RSC cache key manipulation + Server Action body confusion. Affects 13.x-15.x App Router.

rsc_cache_key_manipulation.description: RSC responses keyed on URL + cookie subset. Inject headers that the cache strips but origin reflects — poisoned response served to subsequent users.

isr_revalidate_poison.description: On-demand revalidation tokens — if revalidate endpoint is unauthenticated, attacker forces stale-content replacement.

probes.description: Revalidation endpoint fingerprint — common Next.js handler pattern

server_action_body_confusion.description: Server Action POST body cached because handler returns 200 + cacheable headers. Subsequent users see attacker action result.

probes.description: Detect Server Action that returns cacheable response (header anomaly)

i18n_middleware_strip_bypass_2026.description: Next.js 15 i18n middleware bypass May 2026 wave (CVE-2026-23864/23869/23870 class) — stripping locale prefix /en/ in middleware-rewrite rules causes protected route to render without auth check.

probes.description: GET /en/admin then GET /admin without /en — both should redirect to login; if /admin 200, i18n strip bypassed middleware

ws_upgrade_ssrf_2026.description: Next.js WebSocket Upgrade SSRF (CVE-2026-44578 class) — server-side fetch for proxy upgrade does not filter loopback or cloud-metadata Host headers. Confirm via canary echo in response or Collaborator DNS hit.

probes.description: Send WebSocket upgrade with Host: 169.254.169.254 — if upgrade succeeds or IMDS marker echoes, SSRF confirmed

next_image_unbounded_cache_dos_2026.description: Next.js /_next/image unbounded cache DoS (CVE-2026-27980 class) — repeated requests with q=1..100 generate uncached variants; resource exhaustion.

probes.description: Hit /_next/image?url=/<asset>&w=128&q=N for N in 1..10 sequentially — if no 429/cache header, unbounded variant generation
